data.title: Chunghwa Telecom reports 2026 guidance
data.description:
• Chunghwa Telecom (CHT) announced on Friday that it issued its consolidated financial guidance for 2026.
  • The company expects 2026 total revenue to rise by NT$5.88B  to NT$7.57B, or 2.5% to 3.2%, to between NT$241.99B and NT$243.68B, compared with unaudited 2025 results.
  • Operating costs and expenses in 2026 are expected to rise by NT$6.54B to NT$7.01B, or 3.5% to 3.7%, to between NT$193.99B and NT$194.46B from a year earlier.
  • Operating income ranges between NT$48.14B and NT$49.66B, representing a YoY change of  -0.8% to +2.3%.
  • The company said capital spending on property, plant, and equipment in 2026 is expected to rise by NT$4.07B to NT$31.91B from a year earlier.
  • Income before income tax and net income attributable to stockholders are expected to be NT$48.71B to NT$50.23B NT$37.39B to NT$38.94B, respectively.
  • Net earnings per share are expected to be NT$4.82B to NT$5.02B.
  • The consensus EPS estimate is $1.66 and revenue of $7.62B.

data.title: Darden Restaurants attracts an upgrade from Mizuho on Olive Garden strength
data.description:
Mizuho Securities turned more constructive on Darden Restaurants (DRI) on the expectation for same-store sales and margin improvement. The firm moved to an Outperform rating on Darden (DRI) after having it set at Neutral,

Analyst Nick Setyan and his team expect the casual dining segment to outperform this year and view Darden (DRI) as a beneficiary of segment tailwinds and company-specific drivers. Following a series of channel checks, Setyan boosted his estimate for Olive Garden's FQ3 comparable sales to 5.0% growth vs. the consensus estimate of 4.5%. The forecasts for FY26 and FY27 Olive Garden comparable sales were also boosted. Restaurant-level margins are also anticipated to improve amid cooling inflation and elevated menu pricing. Finally, the stepped-up pace of unit growth is seen supporting the EBITDA and EPS growth algorithms.

Mizuho increased its price target on Darden (DRI) to $235 from $195. 

Darden Restaurants (DRI) traded flat at $205.15 in midday action on Friday. The restaurant stock is up more than 11% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Fortinet in focus as TD Cowen upgrades on back of 'solid checks'
data.description:
Cybersecurity company Fortinet (FTNT) was in the spotlight on Friday as investment firm TD Cowen upgraded shares, citing “solid checks.”

Shares jumped 7% in late morning trading.

“[We] upgrade FTNT to Buy based on 1) solid checks suggesting stability as FY26 unfolds; while keeping our model intact, we see upside to our 4Q25 billings/ [revenue estimates] (11.6%/13.3% y/y) and see our FY26 billings [estimate] of 11.6% revised higher on stable spending as AI is not eating security software but rather augmenting it; [valuation] at sub 7x EV/FY27 rev vs. potential low/mid-teen FY26 growth looks reasonable,” analyst Shaul Eyal wrote in a note to clients. 

Eyal raised his rating on Fortinet to Buy from Hold and put a $100 price target on the stock.

Delving deeper, Eyal said the recent negative investor sentiment stems from “soft” channel checks, concerns about rising memory prices, a fading refresh cycle, “lukewarm” at best sentiment on the broader software sector, and the fact that Fortinet is not viewed “as a genuine platform provider.” However, Eyal pushed back on that narrative.

“Our recent 2026 survey points to positive demand backdrop for FTNT's core markets,” Eyal explained. “Network Security remains a top budget priority, cited by the majority of respondents (N-160) as both firewall and SASE are expected to see broad budget expansion as customers shift to adopt hybrid-related platforms. Gen-AI ramp remains a driver.”

data.title: NextNRG terminates at-the-market sales agreement
data.description:
• NextNRG (NXXT) (NASDAQ:NXXT) on Friday said that it has terminated its at-the-market [ATM] sales agreement with ThinkEquity LLC, H.C. Wainwright & Co., and Roth Capital Partners, effective January 17, 2026.
  • The agreement had allowed for potential issuance and sale of shares under an ATM offering.
  • NextNRG said it has no immediate plans for another ATM offering and is focusing on strategic investors to support long-term growth and operational expansion.
  • NXXT is -4.01% to $1.075.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Infinitum proposes 10-for-1 share consolidation
data.description:
• Infinitum Copper (INFI:CA) on Friday said that its board has approved a proposed 10-for-1 consolidation of its common shares, subject to acceptance by the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • The company said the consolidation will also apply to outstanding convertible securities, including stock options and warrants, with proportionate adjustments to exercise and conversion prices.
  • No fractional shares will be issued, and the company’s name and trading symbol are not expected to change.
  • Infinitum said the effective date will be announced following TSXV approval.
  • INFI:CA is -20.0% to $0.04.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bain Capital Specialty Finance prices $350 million senior notes due 2031
data.description:
• Bain Capital Specialty Finance (BCSF) on Friday said that it has priced an offering of $350 million aggregate principal amount of 5.950% senior notes due March 1, 2031.
  • The company said the notes may be redeemed at par plus a make-whole premium, or at par one month prior to maturity.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 29, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • Proceeds are expected to be used to repay outstanding secured debt and for general corporate purposes.
  • BCSF is -0.18% to $13.685.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Starbucks Investor Day and FQ1 to spotlight turnaround progress
data.description:
Starbucks’ (SBUX) Investor Day on January 29 and fiscal first quarter  results the day prior will likely shed light on the company’s efforts to reinvigorate the business under CEO Brian Niccol and momentum behind its “Back to Starbucks” initiative.

Analysts are expecting to see improving sales trends, cost savings, and for Investor Day, a focus on FY26 guidance and 3-year targets.

“We believe the event could be a positive catalyst for shares given recent sales momentum,” UBS analyst Dennis Geiger said in a note to clients.

More immediately, fiscal first quarter results could show the first domestic comparable sales gain in two years, which should set the brand up for a return to positive full-year comparable sales gains in FY26, says William Blair’s Sharon Zackfia.

She expects 1% comp gains (versus +2% consensus estimates) for the first quarter and 2% growth for the full year (versus 3% estimates), translating into continued improvement in two-year comp trends, while UBS projects a more optimistic increase of 3% to 4% in comp sales for the first quarter. This likely reflects improved operations, effective marketing, easier comparisons to previous quarters, contributions from the holiday menu, and a “notable” uplift from recent store closures.

However, the bigger question for investors at this point is margin recovery.

Zackfia cites the 13.4% decline in Americas margin in FY25 before half a billion dollars of incremental labor expenses expected in FY26 for continued concerns over profitability.

UBS’s Geiger also sees margin pressures extending in the near to medium term, reflecting increased labor hours, rollout of the Assistant Store Manager program, and investments in the Green Apron service model.

Offsets should come in the form of cost savings as well as supply chain efficiencies, improving transaction and sales growth, all of which are “key factors” to strengthening margins.

“We continue to believe the ‘Back to Starbucks’ plan should support a sales and earnings recovery,” Geiger says, with tailwinds from store closures and existing and new sales initiatives supporting U.S. same store sales inflection and momentum going forward.

Zackfia sees a path to consolidated operating margin recovery “within spitting distance of 2023 levels by 2030, implying a 15% to 20% EPS CAGR over the next five years,” she notes.

Accordingly, Geiger maintains his Neutral rating on Starbucks (SBUX) until there is better visibility into the recovery trajectory in sales growth, margin improvement, and earnings upside.

William Blair’s Zackfia, however, upgraded the coffee chain to Outperform from Market Perform to reflect the “optimism already baked in given the stock’s 15% year-to-date increase."

"We believe a credible case exists for the stock to reach more than $140 by 2029, assuming a 30x P/E multiple off a 2030 estimate of more than $4.70, implying the opportunity for a ~10% CAGR in share appreciation over the next four years,” she notes.

Shares of Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) are up 1.5% on Friday, adding to a 15% gain since the beginning of 2026.

data.title: Flexible Solutions reports fourth-quarter and full year 2025 revenue
data.description:
• Flexible Solutions (FSI) on Friday said that fourth-quarter revenue was $9.10 million, up from $8.84 million in the same period a year earlier.
  • The company said full-year 2025 revenue increased to $38.50 million from $38.23 million in 2024.
  • Flexible Solutions said sales growth in 2025 came during a transition year that included construction of its Panama factory and retooling of its Illinois facility.
  • The company said agriculture markets were weak throughout the year, while progress continued in food-grade products.
  • Consensus for 2025 revenue is $41.08M.
  • Flexible Solutions said complete financial results will be released after market close on March 31, 2026, with a conference call scheduled for April 1, 2026.
  • FSI is -0.43% to $6.143.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Shell, partners eye potential $20B to develop Nigeria oil project
data.description:
Shell (SHEL) and its partners are exploring how to develop the Bonga South West deepwater oilfield offshore Nigeria, which could see them spend as much as $20B on the project, Shell CEO Wael Sawan said following a meeting with Nigerian President Tinubu.

Half of the $20B would be capital and the other half would come from operating expenses and other spending that would go into the country, Sawan said, adding Shell (SHEL) also is interested in Nigeria's exploration license round.

Tinubu said he has approved "investment-linked" incentives for Shell (SHEL) to develop the long-stalled project, but "my expectation is clear: Bonga South West must reach a final investment decision within the first term of this administration," which ends in May 2027.

Shell (SHEL) acquired a stake from TotalEnergies (TTE) last year that raised its share in the Bonga oilfield to 65%, underlining its continued interest in offshore Nigeria production after it sold its onshore assets; other Bonga shareholders include units of Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Eni (E), as well as state-owned Nigerian National Petroleum.

Bonga South West in Nigeria's oil-producing Niger Delta is estimated to contain 820M barrels of reserves with a potential peak production capacity of 220K bbl/day.

data.title: La Rosa Holdings reports preliminary 2025 revenue of ~$79M, Up 14% YoY (LRHC)
data.description:
• La Rosa Holdings (LRHC) reported preliminary, unaudited fiscal year 2025 revenue of ~$79M.
  • The result represents an estimated 14% year-over-year increase compared with fiscal 2024 revenue.
  • LRHC shares down 9.8%.
